title: NOC 10019 – Other Administrative Services Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Other Administrative Services Managers classified under NOC 10019 fall within one of the most senior and versatile occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals oversee core administrative operations that keep organizations running efficiently—making them attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate administrative-service departments
Lead and advise teams handling records, security, finance, purchasing, HR, and related services
Plan and manage budgets for contracts, equipment, and supplies
Prepare performance reports and briefs for executive committees
Recruit, train, and supervise administrative staff
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Over the period 2024–2033, Canada is projected to create 4,600 new positions for Other Administrative Services Managers, while approximately 4,800 job seekers may enter the occupation. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this offers encouraging opportunities when supported by a strong immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Other Administrative Services Managers are in high demand across Canada’s major sectors, including finance, human resources, purchasing, regulatory compliance, and corporate administration. Demand under this TEER category expanded strongly following the pandemic and is expected to remain steady over the coming years.
Strong hiring prospects across provinces and territories
Competitive compensation compared to similar administrative leadership roles
Positive immigration prospects for experienced overseas professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $37,050 to $77,706, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Why Other Administrative Services Managers Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10019 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
What Is NOC 10019 – Other Administrative Services Managers?

NOC 10019 is a senior management classification under Canada’s National Occupational Classification (NOC 2021). It includes professionals who plan, organize, direct, and evaluate administrative services supporting an organization’s daily operations.

These managers may oversee records management, security services, governance and compliance functions, procurement coordination, HR administration, and other operational departments—ensuring efficiency, legal compliance, and cost control.
benefits: In addition to salary, many employers provide:
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
